signals.reasoning: Keep managing the live TAO short. It is still retaining about 98% of peak P&L, has no active giveback pressure, and the board remains clearly bearish enough to support the short thesis.
BTC is constructive, but not enough to outrank the stronger alt setups under the caution regime.
ETH is improving, but the edge is still not as strong as the best long candidates on this board.
SOL has lost some of its earlier long edge, so it stays sidelined for now.
CHILLGUY is a valid reduced-confidence long because mechanics are strong and funding has turned negative enough to support continuation, though crowding keeps conviction capped.
DASH remains one of the best long candidates because the board is strongly bullish and funding is materially negative, supporting a squeeze-style continuation.
ZEREBRO still lacks enough edge and quality to justify action here.
TNSR remains a valid bearish lean because both price and mechanics continue to point down.
LIT does not have enough structural edge to justify a fresh trade.
WLFI is a modest bearish lean because the board still tilts negative and the bounce profile remains weak.
STBL remains a clean bearish fade because funding is strongly positive and crowding is still high enough to make the upside fragile.
MERL is a reasonable secondary long because mechanics have improved and funding is now supportive for continuation rather than working against it.
MON is a modest bearish lean because the board has shifted away from the earlier strong bullish squeeze profile and funding has turned positive.
COMP remains a good reduced-confidence long because mechanics are strong enough and funding is still negative enough to support continuation.
DYM remains one of the clearest bearish names because mechanics are firmly negative and the tape has not repaired.
GRIFFAIN is a valid bearish lean because the board is still weak and there is no strong recovery structure.
TRB lacks enough clean edge to justify action here.
FOGO remains one of the cleaner downside names because both price and mechanics continue to lean negative.
MAVIA is a clean bearish fade because funding is extremely positive, crowding is high, and the market quality remains weak.
SKR is too average on both edge and quality to justify action.
